text_post:
At #CES2026, we previewed how our next-gen Gemini for Google TV experience is making TV even more helpful and interactive. Over the coming months, you’ll be able to:

 Enjoy a more visual search experience that delivers tailored responses with relevant images or videos
 Search your Google Photos library for specific people or moments — then apply artistic styles with Photos Remix or transform them into cinematic slideshows
 Use Nano Banana and Veo to reimagine your personal photos or create original media directly on your TV
 Skip complicated settings menus and simply tell Gemini things like “the screen is too dim” or “the dialogue is lost” to adjust your picture and sound without leaving your movie or show

Look for these features coming to select TCL devices first and other Google TV devices over the coming months.

Want to take your pictures from good to great? It’s all about taking advantage of your phone’s camera settings. Here are some key features the Pixel Camera team used to capture Lunar New Year and the Year of the Horse celebrations in Jiufen, Taiwan .

• Try Pixel’s Long Exposure mode, which simulates a long-exposure capture without the need for a tripod or low-light conditions.
• To get incredible detail, even in low-light conditions, use Night Sight.
• Take advantage of Pixel 10 Pro’s 5x telephoto lens, which has a long focal length that can create a compression effect where background and foreground subjects — like rows of red lanterns — look closer together.

That’s more than double the performance of 3
text_post:
Today, we’re releasing Gemini 3.1 Pro.

It’s the same core intelligence that powers Gemini 3 Deep Think, now scaled for your practical applications. It’s a smarter model for your most complex tasks.

See 3.1 Pro in action ↓

text:
We asked Gemini 3.1 Pro to build a real-time ISS tracking dashboard. 

To pull this off, the model had to reason through several logic streams at once to map out a plan:

 Orchestrating live telemetry from a public API.
 Building a responsive UI with perfect data sync.

 Keep track of your music and see your playing history with the Now Playing app. 
 Access more security features and safety tools on Pixel Watch.

text: To pressure test Pixel 10 Pro Fold, we have racks of machines designed to open and close the devices repeatedly. The machines go through thousands of cycles at a time to verify that each Pixel 10 Fold Pro can handle 10+ years of folding.
text_post:
Wonder what goes down in a 
@MadebyGoogle
 Pixel lab? We’re giving you a behind-the-scenes peek at how we test our phones for reliability. Let’s go 

We use this machine to simulate a Pixel getting tossed around inside a bag. From there, our team checks for abrasions while adding or removing things you may find in your Pixel rubbing up against, like hand sanitizer, a pen or keys.

text: To ensure Pixel 10 phones can withstand falls, we use this machine to drop Pixel phones from various heights and angles on different types of surfaces like concrete, over and over again.

This means it can process different types of media in one unified embedding space, and intuitively understand how they relate and connect to each other across 100+ languages.

For example, it doesn’t just see "a video of a dog" — it understands how that video relates to the word "puppy" and the sound of a bark, all at the same time. 

Start building today via the Gemini API and Vertex AI.
